title: Repository convention profile extraction
description: Detect .editorconfig, linter/formatter config, directory layout, and package-manager/build-tooling conventions from a repository; normalize into a ConventionProfile knowledge record with provenance (observed vs inferred per §7.4). Enables Petruk to conform to existing conventions during implementation and to use a reference repo as a baseline when scaffolding a new project. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §2.7, §13.5, §27, Milestone 2.
close_reason: internal/convention.Extract() detects .editorconfig, linter/formatter config (eslint/prettier/golangci-lint/gofmt/stylelint/rustfmt), package manager, monorepo-vs-single layout, and directory naming convention, normalizing into a convention-profile KnowledgeRecord with §27.4-conformant observed/inferred provenance. Scoped to detection only; applying a profile (§27.5) and baseline scaffolding (§27.6) deferred to M3. Covered by 4 tests.

title: RTK integration
description: Deliverable of M1: Core runtime and local repository workflow. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
close_reason: internal/tools/rtk.go: Supervisor.RunViaRTK()/RTKAvailable() run commands through the user's rtk CLI for token-compressed output (§11.4, §2.1). 2 tests, run for real against installed rtk (skips gracefully if rtk absent). Verified: go test, lint, build pass.

title: ripgrep integration
description: Deliverable of M1: Core runtime and local repository workflow. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
close_reason: internal/tools/ripgrep.go: Supervisor.Search() runs rg through the supervised execution path (§14.1). 2 tests, run for real against installed rg (skips gracefully if rg absent, e.g. in an environment without it). Verified: go test, lint, build pass.

title: Evidence bundle skeleton
description: Deliverable of M1: Core runtime and local repository workflow. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
close_reason: internal/evidence/bundle.go: NewBundle() creates §17.2 skeleton (.punakawan/evidence/<runID>/<taskID>/ with logs/screenshots/traces subdirs) + Path() helper. Content-writing (task.yaml, diff.patch etc) deferred to M6 execution runtime. 2 tests. Verified: go test, lint, build pass.

title: JSONL event journal
description: Deliverable of M1: Core runtime and local repository workflow. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
close_reason: internal/evidence/journal.go: append-only JSONL Journal (§7.5, §19.1) using generated protocol.Event, under .punakawan/runs/<runID>/events.jsonl. Append/List. 2 tests. Verified: go test, lint, build pass.

title: Worktree lifecycle
description: Deliverable of M1: Core runtime and local repository workflow. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
close_reason: internal/gitops/worktree.go: WorktreeManager implements §11.1 steps 1-3+10 (lock, clean-check, worktree+branch creation, removal) gated by approval (§16) via RequestApproval/Approve/Deny using the existing approvals.Store. No dedicated worktree-creation category exists in §16.1's approval list, so it uses ApprovalRecordOperationDestructiveFilesystemAction as the closest fit (documented in code). Non-blocking file-based per-repo lock (§3.1 workspace locking). Reuses gitops.Inspector.Status for the clean-repo check rather than duplicating git-status parsing. 5 tests: unapproved-create-fails, full request->approve->create->remove happy path, dirty-repo refusal, denied-request refusal, concurrent-lock refusal. Verified: go test, lint, build all pass.

title: Git repository inspection
description: Deliverable of M1: Core runtime and local repository workflow. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
close_reason: Implemented internal/gitops/inspect.go: Inspector wraps git status/log/diff/rev-parse through tools.Supervisor (read-only, no writes). All operations go through Supervisor.Run for dir-allowlist/timeout/output-cap enforcement. Added inspect_test.go with real git repo fixtures (2 commits + uncommitted change). go build/vet/gofmt/test all clean.

title: Staleness detection
description: Deliverable of M2: Knowledge and task foundation. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
close_reason: internal/knowledge/staleness.go: ContentHash() produces schema-compliant sha256 digests; Store.CheckStale compares a record's stored source.content_hash against a caller-supplied current hash and transitions validity.state to stale on mismatch via Put. No stored hash means no baseline, so it declines to guess. Covered by 5 tests.

title: Knowledge import and export
description: Deliverable of M2: Knowledge and task foundation. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
close_reason: Store.Export/Import in internal/knowledge/importexport.go: JSONL export ordered by id for reproducibility, fail-fast import that routes every record through Put so §7.3/§7.4 provenance validation applies uniformly. Covered by TestExportImportRoundTrip and two rejection tests.

title: Tool supervisor
description: Deliverable of M1: Core runtime and local repository workflow. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
close_reason: internal/tools/supervisor.go: Supervisor.Run() enforces §11.4 - explicit argv only (no shell), working-dir allowlist, env allowlist (default PATH/HOME/LANG/TMPDIR), per-call timeout via context, output cap via limitedBuffer (truncates, doesn't error), process-group termination on cancel (Setpgid + SIGTERM to -pid, 5s WaitDelay before SIGKILL). POSIX-only, documented; multi-platform deferred to M9. 6 tests including a real timeout test (bug found+fixed: context-cancellation check must run before exec.ExitError check, since a SIGTERM-killed process also surfaces as ExitError). Verified: go test, lint, build pass.

title: Approval storage
description: Deliverable of M1: Core runtime and local repository workflow. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
close_reason: internal/approvals: JSONL-backed Store (append-only per §7.5) using generated protocol.ApprovalRecord (§16.2). Append/List (full history)/Current (folds to latest per id)/Pending. 2 tests covering request->resolve lifecycle. Verified: go test, lint, build all pass.

title: Cross-repository relations
description: Deliverable of M2: Knowledge and task foundation. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
close_reason: Added knowledge_relations index table synced transactionally on Put/Delete; Store.Related(id) does the reverse-lookup join to find records that relate to a given id across repositories. Covered by TestKnowledgeRelationsRoundTrip.

title: Policy engine
description: Deliverable of M1: Core runtime and local repository workflow. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
close_reason: internal/policy: Capabilities struct matching §15.1 (filesystem/git/external/browser/execution), Level enum normalizes both §15.1 informal vocab (allowed/approval/denied) and §16.3 canonical vocab (allow/require-approval/deny/allow-with-constraints) via UnmarshalYAML. Default() is conservative (push+default-branch-write denied, external writes require approval). AllowsFilesystemWrite() enforces deny-globs (doublestar, supports **) over write-globs. 3 tests. Verified: go test, lint, build all pass.

title: Requirement-to-task mapping
description: Deliverable of M2: Knowledge and task foundation. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
close_reason: internal/tasks.CreateTaskForRequirement builds a TaskContract from a requirement KnowledgeRecord per §10.1 (requirement_id from Id, jira_key from source.external_id when provider=jira), creates the tracking Beads issue via internal/beads, and persists a tracked-by relation back onto the requirement via Store.Put. WireDependency translates the schema's blocks/discovered-from/requires vocabulary onto bd dep add's types. Covered by 6 tests including a full end-to-end round trip.

title: Workspace discovery
description: Deliverable of M1: Core runtime and local repository workflow. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
close_reason: internal/workspace: Discover() walks up for .punakawan/workspace.yaml (git-style), Load() parses+validates per §6 schema (version check, duplicate/empty id checks). RepositoryPath()/PolicyPath() accessors. 5 tests against test/fixtures/workspace/. Verified: go test, lint, build all pass.

title: Beads integration
description: Deliverable of M2: Knowledge and task foundation. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
close_reason: internal/beads wraps the subset of the bd CLI needed (create, dep add, --version) via Supervisor, mirroring the internal/tools/rtk.go thin-wrapper style. Flag choices (--json, --acceptance newline-joined) verified empirically against bd --help. Covered by 4 tests.

title: Provenance model
description: Deliverable of M2: Knowledge and task foundation. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
close_reason: Validate() enforces §7.3/§7.4 provenance fields (id scheme, type, title, source, extraction.method, validity.state, verified-requires-verified_by) and is wired into Store.Put(). Covered by provenance_test.go and Put-rejection tests in service_test.go.

title: CLI
description: Deliverable of M1: Core runtime and local repository workflow. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
close_reason: internal/app/app.go (bootstrap: discover workspace, load policy, wire supervisor/approvals/inspector/worktree-manager) + cmd/punakawan/*.go (cobra CLI): 'workspace show', 'git status|log|diff|branch <repo-id>', 'worktree request|approve|deny|create|remove <repo-id> <task-id>', 'doctor'. Manually smoke-tested full worktree lifecycle end-to-end against a real ephemeral workspace+git repo, then codified as 5 in-process CLI tests + 2 app-bootstrap tests. Verified: go test/vet/gofmt, make lint/test/integration-test all pass repo-wide.

title: Knowledge service
description: Deliverable of M2: Knowledge and task foundation. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
close_reason: internal/knowledge/service.go: Store.Put (upsert)/Get/ListByType/Delete over protocol.KnowledgeRecord (generated in M0). ErrNotFound for missing records. 2 tests covering full CRUD + upsert + not-found, run against a real dolt sql-server (skips gracefully if dolt absent). Verified: go test, lint, build pass.

title: Dolt knowledge schema
description: Deliverable of M2: Knowledge and task foundation. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
close_reason: internal/knowledge/dolt.go: Open() ensures a Dolt repo exists at dataDir (dolt init with fixed --name/--email since this is Punakawan's own internal DB, not human-authored), starts dolt sql-server as a supervised background process on a free local port (via new Supervisor.StartBackground), connects via go-sql-driver/mysql, applies schema (single knowledge_records table: id/type/status/validity_state denormalized + full record as JSON data column - avoids 23 separate normalized tables for the heterogeneous §7.1 entity types). Verified: go test with a real dolt sql-server, lint/build pass. Also added dolt install step to CI.

title: Basic CI
description: Deliverable of M0: Architecture and protocol foundation. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
close_reason: .github/workflows/ci.yml: checkout, setup-go+setup-node/pnpm, then make bootstrap/lint/protocol-check/test/integration-test on push+PR. Scoped to what M0 actually built; broader Section 21.1 items (license scan, secret scan, multi-platform smoke build, contract tests) are later-milestone concerns (M9 hardening, adapter contract tests). Verified: workflow YAML parses correctly, and every make target it calls (bootstrap/lint/protocol-check/test/integration-test) passes locally.

title: Threat model
description: Deliverable of M0: Architecture and protocol foundation. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
close_reason: Wrote docs/security/threat-model.md organizing §15/§16/§24 into assets, trust boundaries (Go core trusted / TS adapters semi-trusted+sandboxed / external content untrusted), 7 threat/mitigation subsections, and residual-risk section flagging M9 items (adapter sandboxing, secret broker, tool checksums, recovery) as not yet implemented.

title: Architecture decision records
description: Deliverable of M0: Architecture and protocol foundation. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
close_reason: Created ADR-0001 through ADR-0015 under docs/architecture/ transcribing all 15 decisions from plan §25, plus a README index; each ADR follows the Status/Context/Decision/Consequences structure grounded in the plan's surrounding sections.

title: Adapter manifest format
description: Deliverable of M0: Architecture and protocol foundation. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
close_reason: Created docs/adapters/manifest-format.md documenting every adapter manifest field (id, name, version, protocol, runtime, provides, permissions.network/filesystem/secrets, operations.<name>.side_effect/approval) and how the Go core supervisor uses the manifest across the §5.2 lifecycle (start -> initialize -> validate manifest -> grant capabilities -> execute -> receive result -> record evidence -> stop/reuse), referencing protocol/adapter.schema.json as canonical. Created examples/atlassian-playwright/adapter-manifest.example.yaml, the verbatim §5.4 Atlassian Cloud manifest. Validated with a throwaway node script (js-yaml + ajv/dist/2020 + ajv-formats installed via npm install --no-save in scratch dir): YAML parses cleanly and passes ajv.compile(schema)/validate(doc) against protocol/adapter.schema.json with zero errors. No protocol/*.schema.json files modified.

title: JSON-RPC stdio prototype
description: Deliverable of M0: Architecture and protocol foundation. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
close_reason: Newline-delimited JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio. TS side: packages/adapter-sdk/src/stdio.ts (serveStdio helper) + prototypeAdapter.ts (initialize validates against generated AdapterManifestSchema, execute/sleep supports cancellation, shutdown exits). Go side: internal/adapters/stdio.go (Client: Start/Call/Shutdown/Kill, ctx-based cancellation sends a cancel notification). Integration test test/integration/adapter_stdio_test.go (build-tag integration): TestInitializeHandshake proves Go can start+handshake a TS adapter with schema validation; TestExecuteCancellation proves a 150ms ctx timeout aborts a 5s sleep op promptly. Verified: make lint/test/integration-test all pass. Satisfies M0 acceptance criteria on adapter start + cancellation/timeout.

title: Generated Go and TypeScript types
description: Deliverable of M0: Architecture and protocol foundation. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
close_reason: Go codegen: go-jsonschema as go.mod tool dep + //go:generate directive in pkg/protocol/doc.go -> types_generated.go (44 types). TS codegen: json-schema-to-typescript + json-schema-to-zod in packages/schema-types/scripts/generate.mjs -> src/generated/*.ts + *.zod.ts (interfaces + Zod validators per §5.5), tsconfig+build/typecheck wired. Makefile generate/protocol-check targets wired (protocol-check re-generates and git-diffs to catch staleness). Verified: go build/vet, tsc --noEmit, make generate/build/lint/test all pass. Note: json-schema-to-zod (StefanTerdell) is dev-tooling-only (no runtime exposure) but flagged unmaintained as of its own README; fine for now, revisit if it breaks.

title: Canonical JSON Schemas
description: Deliverable of M0: Architecture and protocol foundation. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
close_reason: 8 canonical protocol schemas (adapter, event, workflow, knowledge, task, evidence, approval, flow) written to protocol/*.schema.json, draft 2020-12, grounded in plan §5.4, §7, §9-10, §12.9, §16-19. Validated: valid JSON, ajv-cli schema compile clean (date-time format warnings are ajv-cli plugin config only, not schema defects).

title: Go and pnpm workspaces
description: Deliverable of M0: Architecture and protocol foundation. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
close_reason: package.json + pnpm-workspace.yaml + per-package stubs (11 packages), Makefile targets. Verified: pnpm install, make build/test/lint/bootstrap all pass.

title: Repository scaffolding
description: Deliverable of M0: Architecture and protocol foundation. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
close_reason: Monorepo tree, go.mod/go.work, README/LICENSE/SECURITY/CONTRIBUTING, .gitignore. Verified: go build, go vet, gofmt clean.

title: M2: Knowledge and task foundation
description: Dolt knowledge schema, provenance model, Beads integration, requirement-to-task mapping, cross-repository relations. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
acceptance_criteria: A requirement can link to files and tests across repositories; every relation retains source provenance; Beads tasks map back to requirements and Jira keys; knowledge changes can be diffed and versioned; a repository's editorconfig, formatter/linter config, and directory conventions are captured as a ConventionProfile with provenance.
close_reason: M2 complete: all 9 deliverables done (Dolt schema, knowledge service, provenance model, cross-repository relations, Beads integration, requirement-to-task mapping, import/export, staleness detection, convention profile extraction). Full make lint && make test green.

title: M1: Core runtime and local repository workflow
description: CLI, workspace discovery, policy engine, approval storage, tool supervisor, Git worktree lifecycle, JSONL event journal. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
acceptance_criteria: Punakawan opens a workspace with multiple repositories; it can inspect repositories without writing; it can create an isolated worktree after approval; every command is auditable and cancellable; default-branch writes are blocked.
close_reason: All 11 deliverables closed. Acceptance criteria verified via CLI: workspace opens with multiple repositories (workspace show), repositories inspected without writing (git status/log/diff/branch, read-only through Supervisor), isolated worktree created after approval (worktree request/approve/create, refuses without approval), every command auditable/cancellable (Supervisor timeout+process-group kill, tested; JSONL event journal available), default-branch writes blocked (policy Default() denies git.default_branch_write and git.push requires approval).

title: M0: Architecture and protocol foundation
description: Repository scaffolding, Go/pnpm workspaces, canonical JSON Schema protocol, JSON-RPC stdio prototype. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
acceptance_criteria: Go can start a TypeScript adapter; both sides validate the same message schemas; cancellation and timeout work; CI detects stale generated code.
close_reason: All 9 deliverables closed. Acceptance criteria verified: Go starts a TS adapter (stdio prototype), both sides validate the same schemas (generated Go structs + Zod validators, manifest validated in TestInitializeHandshake), cancellation/timeout work (TestExecuteCancellation), CI detects stale generated code (protocol-check in ci.yml).

title: MCP server for role invocation
description: punakawan mcp serve (Go, official modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk, stdio transport): exposes semar/gareng/petruk/bagong prompts + build_context_dossier/submit_*_review/submit_*_plan/get_workflow_state/advance_workflow tools. Connected MCP client (e.g. Claude Code) performs the actual reasoning; Punakawan validates+persists results. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §28, ADR-0016.

title: Workflow checkpoints
description: Deliverable of M3: Semar, Gareng, and Petruk planning workflow. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
close_reason: internal/workflow persists WorkflowRun state via append-only JSONL, mirroring internal/approvals' Store/Current pattern (§18.2's 'load last durable checkpoint'). New() creates the initial 'created' checkpoint; Advance() appends a checkpoint and updates state, rejecting unknown states and any move out of a terminal state (completed/failed/cancelled) but not enforcing an unstated transition graph, since §18.1 only enumerates states, not a specific edge set. Covered by 7 tests.

Covered by 4 tests.

title: M8: Bagong independent review
description: Independent context builder, requirement coverage analysis, diff/test/API/migration/E2E review, finding severity model, reopen-task workflow. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
acceptance_criteria: Bagong reviews raw sources and diffs independently; missing acceptance criteria are visible; high-severity findings reopen or create tasks; delivery cannot be marked complete with unresolved blocking findings.

title: M7: Playwright test generation and adjustment
description: Flow-to-Playwright compiler, parameterization, fixture integration, assertion generation, existing-test matching. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
acceptance_criteria: A recorded flow generates a valid Playwright test; existing tests can be updated rather than duplicated; generated tests use semantic locators; test reports and traces become evidence.

title: M6: Plan-to-Beads and Petruk execution
description: Task graph generator, ready-task selection, worktree executor, diff checker, test execution, OpenAPI compatibility check, discovered-task handling. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
acceptance_criteria: An approved plan becomes dependency-aware tasks; Petruk executes only ready tasks; each task has isolated changes and evidence; scope expansion becomes a new task; failed checks prevent task completion.

title: M9: Hardening and release
description: Recovery after interruption, adapter sandboxing, secret broker, tool installer/checksums, OpenTelemetry, SBOM, signed release artifacts. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
acceptance_criteria: Interrupted runs recover safely; orphan child processes are terminated; tool binaries are checksum-verified; secrets are excluded from logs and evidence; release artifacts work on supported platforms.
